content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      :    Advice Files - Sections 84308, 87101          Date    :    May 2, 1985
                                                            No. M-85-100


From    :    FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
             Diane Fishburn [signature]

Subject :    Section 84308 and Rule of Legally Required Participation


             I advised Nancy Miller, lawyer for the Sacramento Transit
             Development Agency (Light Rail), that the rule of legally
             required participation in Section 87101 applies to the situation
             where 3 of the 5 governing board members were disqualified
             pursuant to Section 84308.

             DMF:plh

State of California

M e m o r a n d u m

To     :  Advice Files - Sections 84308, 87101              Date  :  May 2, 1985
                                                                    No. M-85-100


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
          Diane Fishburn

Subject:  Section 84308 and Rule of Legally Required Participation


          I advised Nancy Miller, lawyer for the Sacramento Transit
Development Agency (Light Rail), that the rule of legally
required participation in Section 87101 applies to the situation
where 3 of the 5 governing board members were disqualified
pursuant to Section 84308.

DMP:plh

Randal Friedman
1360 Kelton #2
Los Angeles, CA 90024


Barbara Millman, Chief
Legal Division
Fair Political Pratices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Millman:

     In response to a conversation between Mr. John Harrington and Bob
Leidigh, wherein Senator Rosenthal requested my participation in the
application of Mr. Murphy Dunne before the California Coastal Commission,
I am seeking clarification of my participation pursuant to Section 83114(b)
of the Government Code. This application has been scheduled before the
Coastal Commission on September 29, 1983 so your prompt response would
be appreciated.

     I was an employee of the Coastal Commission for three years. The first
two years I was on the Permit Appeals staff in the San Francisco office, during
which time I handled several major permits in the Malibu coastal zone. The
last year of my employment I was assigned to the South Coast District and
worked primarily on Malibu projects, including the Malibu Local Coastal
Program. One of the cases I worked on was Murphy Dunne's previous application
before the Commission, also for a single-family residence of a slightly
different design.

     My involvement in this matter is pursuant to a request from the Senator.
I am not under contract with Mr. Dunne, nor am I receiving any compensation
for my work or appearance before the Commission. I have not been designated
in any way as such on the application for a coastal development permit.

     It is my understanding based upon a conversation with Mr. Harrington
of Senator Rosenthal's office that there does not exist any conflict of
interest given these circumstances. It is my desire, however, to have a
formal opinion rendered by the FPPC prior to the hearing before the Coastal
Commission so as to not cast any doubt about this serious question.

     Again, your prompt response given the short amount of time remaining
would be most appreciated.


                                        Very truly yours,

                                        Randal Friedman

sections.question: In response to a conversation between Mr. John Harrington and Bob Leidigh, wherein Senator Rosenthal requested my participation in the application of Mr. Murphy Dunne before the California Coastal Commission, I am seeking clarification of my participation pursuant to Section 83114(b) of the Government Code.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the requestor's participation in a Murphy Dunne application before the California Coastal Commission, requested by Senator Rosenthal, constitute a conflict of interest under Section 83114(b), given the requestor's prior three years of employment at the Coastal Commission including work on Dunne's previous application?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains mixed content: two internal FPPC memoranda (M-85-100) followed by an incoming request letter. The incoming request letter is the primary substantive document. No FPPC response/conclusion is included in this document. The memoranda reference advice given but do not contain full Q/C sections. Document quality is degraded with some OCR artifacts visible.

embedding.summary: This document contains both internal FPPC memoranda regarding Section 84308 and the rule of legally required participation, and an incoming request letter from Randal Friedman seeking clarification on whether his participation in a Coastal Commission matter at Senator Rosenthal's request presents a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 83114(b).
